name: Meaningful Naming
description: Variables, functions, and classes should have meaningful names that reflect their purpose. Single-letter names are allowed only for simple loop counters.

name: Function Size Control
description: Each function should perform a single logical task and should not be excessively long. Fail if functions try to do multiple unrelated things or are clearly oversized.

name: Avoid Magic Numbers
description: Hard-coded numeric values should be replaced with named constants unless they are universally obvious (e.g., 0, 1).

name: Proper Error Handling
description: The code should handle possible error conditions or invalid inputs gracefully instead of crashing or behaving unpredictably.

name: No Unnecessary Global State
description: Global variables or shared state should be avoided unless there is a clear and justified reason.

name: Clear Control Flow
description: The control flow (loops, conditionals, returns) should be easy to follow and not overly complex or deeply nested.

name: Adequate Comments and Documentation
description: Non-obvious logic, complex steps, or important decisions should be explained using comments or documentation.

name: Consistent Coding Style
description: The code should follow a consistent formatting and style (indentation, spacing, naming style) throughout the file.

name: No Redundant or Dead Code
description: Unused variables, unreachable code, or commented-out code blocks should not be present.

name: Proper Input and Output Handling
description: Inputs should be validated where appropriate, and outputs should be clearly formatted and meaningful to the user.
